articles.title: Fix Database Connection Timeout
articles.description: Resolves intermittent database connection timeout issues
articles.solution:
1. Check connection pool settings (increase from 20 to 50)
2. Add connection timeout parameter (30s)
3. Implement connection retry logic with exponential backoff
4. Monitor connection pool usage with metrics
5. Restart services after configuration changes
articles.author: Database Team

articles.title: Database Connection Pool Exhaustion
articles.description: Handles database connection pool exhaustion errors
articles.solution:
1. Increase pool size based on expected concurrent connections
2. Add queue timeout to prevent indefinite waits
3. Enable connection validation on checkout
4. Add monitoring alerts for pool utilization >80%
5. Review and optimize slow queries
articles.author: Database Team

articles.title: High CPU Usage in Patient Service
articles.description: Resolves high CPU utilization in patient data service
articles.solution:
1. Profile application with JProfiler to identify hot spots
2. Optimize query patterns and add database indexes
3. Implement caching layer for frequently accessed data
4. Scale horizontally by adding more service instances
5. Monitor CPU metrics and set autoscaling thresholds
articles.author: Performance Team

articles.title: Memory Leak Detection and Resolution
articles.description: Identifies and fixes memory leaks in Java applications
articles.solution:
1. Generate heap dumps when memory exceeds threshold
2. Analyze with Eclipse MAT to identify leaking objects
3. Check for unclosed resources (connections, streams)
4. Review listener registrations and ensure unsubscribe
5. Add memory monitoring and alerts
articles.author: Infrastructure Team

articles.title: Connection Pool Configuration Best Practices
articles.description: Best practices for configuring database connection pools
articles.solution:
1. Set pool size = (core_count * 2) + effective_spindle_count
2. Connection timeout = 10-30 seconds depending on latency
3. Idle timeout = 10 minutes to clean stale connections
4. Max pool size = 2x average concurrent connections
5. Enable validation queries on checkout/return
articles.author: Database Team

articles.title: Error Rate Spike Investigation
articles.description: Steps to investigate sudden increases in error rates
articles.solution:
1. Check recent deployments and rollback if needed
2. Review application logs for new error patterns
3. Check external service health (DB, APIs, message queues)
4. Monitor resource utilization (CPU, memory, disk)
5. Correlate with traffic spikes or data anomalies
articles.author: SRE Team

articles.title: Response Time Degradation Root Cause
articles.description: Identifies root causes of increased response times
articles.solution:
1. Check database query performance with slow query logs
2. Review network latency with traceroute/ping
3. Monitor external service response times
4. Analyze garbage collection pauses in Java
5. Check for resource contention or throttling
articles.author: Performance Team

articles.title: Reconnection Strategy for Distributed Systems
articles.description: Implements robust reconnection strategies for distributed systems
articles.solution:
1. Implement exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s...)
2. Add jitter to prevent thundering herd
3. Set max retry attempts and timeout
4. Log all reconnection attempts for debugging
5. Monitor reconnection success rates
articles.author: Platform Team

articles.title: Database Query Optimization Techniques
articles.solution:
1. Add indexes on frequently filtered columns
2. Use EXPLAIN PLAN to analyze query execution
3. Avoid SELECT * - specify needed columns
4. Use parameterized queries to enable query caching
5. Archive old data to improve table performance
articles.author: Database Team

articles.title: Load Balancing and Traffic Distribution
articles.description: Configures load balancers for optimal traffic distribution
articles.solution:
1. Use round-robin with health checks every 30s
2. Remove unhealthy instances automatically
3. Implement session affinity if needed
4. Monitor load balancer metrics (request rate, latency)
5. Test failover scenarios regularly
articles.author: Infrastructure Team

articles.title: Application Monitoring and Alerting Setup
articles.description: Sets up comprehensive monitoring and alerting
articles.solution:
1. Monitor key metrics: CPU, memory, error rate, latency
2. Set alerts at 70% for warnings, 85% for critical
3. Configure on-call escalation policies
4. Create runbooks for each alert type
5. Review and tune alerts monthly to reduce noise
articles.author: SRE Team
